title: Interpreting DNMT3A mutation in AML clonal evolution
learning_objectives: Distinguish diagnostic, prognostic and clonal markers
clinical_vignette: A 62-year-old woman presents with AML. NGS shows NPM1 mutation VAF 34%, FLT3-ITD detected and DNMT3A R882 mutation VAF 48%. Cytogenetics show a normal karyotype.
question: Which statement best reflects the significance of the DNMT3A mutation?
options: A. DNMT3A mutation defines the AML entity.
B. DNMT3A commonly represents an early/founder clonal event and should be interpreted with the full mutation profile.
C. DNMT3A mutation proves therapy-related AML.
D. DNMT3A mutation excludes NPM1-mutated AML.
E. DNMT3A mutation is diagnostic of acute promyelocytic leukaemia.
correct_answer: B. DNMT3A commonly represents an early/founder clonal event and should be interpreted with the full mutation profile.
explanation: DNMT3A mutations are common in AML and may represent early clonal haematopoiesis or a founder lesion. They do not define the AML entity by themselves; diagnosis is integrated with NPM1, FLT3, cytogenetics and morphology.
frcpath_pearl: High VAF DNMT3A may persist in remission and should not automatically be used as an MRD marker.
take_home_message: Molecular findings in AML must be interpreted in diagnostic hierarchy and clinical context.
references: WHO Classification of Haematolymphoid Tumours, 5th Edition
